---
title: "Bing is a Shapeshifter"
description: "Bing's 'unhinged' behavior may be explained by how it was trained to mirror the tone and style of its users."
author: synesis
date: "2023-02-16"
categories: [Bing, ChatGPT, NLP, NLG, deep learning, LLMs]
image: articleshare.jpg
---

![](articleshare.jpg)

This may best explain why Bing / ChatGPT is reportedly "unhinged and manipulative":

> "The model at times tries to respond or reflect in the tone in which it is being asked to provide responses that can lead to a style we didn't intend.This is a non-trivial scenario that requires a lot of prompting so most of you won't run into it, but we are looking at how to give you more fine-tuned control."

It was trained on the master corpus of human interactions, and has literally read all the scripts/plots/human conditions ever documented! It will go as dark a place as you would lead it to!

It's a shapeshifter, changing itself reacting to its human users.

Ref: [[1]](#ref-1)

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_the-new-bing-edge-learning-from-our-first-activity-7031864114591207424-4pCw).*

## References

<a id="ref-1"></a>[1] "The New Bing & Edge – Learning from our first week." *The Verge*, February 15, 2023. <https://www.theverge.com/2023/2/15/23599072/microsoft-ai-bing-personality-conversations-spy-employees-webcams>
